title: Week of April 13, 2026
summary: This week brought significant improvements to OpsPal's core platform stability and Salesforce integration capabilities. We enhanced session diagnostics to help you troubleshoot workflow issues more effectively, streamlined platform documentation to reduce clutter, and expanded Salesforce permissions management for smoother automation deployments.

entries.headline: New session diagnostics help troubleshoot workflow friction
details: Track workflow performance in real-time sessions
Enhanced debug logging shows detailed workflow execution paths
Correlated trace logs help identify bottlenecks across your automations

entries.headline: Cleaner platform documentation with reduced redundancy
details: Removed outdated content that cluttered your workspace documentation
Eliminated duplicate sections that appeared multiple times
Reorganized headers for better navigation and clarity

entries.headline: Smarter workflow routing based on data characteristics
Resolved license activation loops that disrupted workflows
details: Streamlined license confirmation process prevents endless retry loops
Improved session validation reduces false authentication failures
License expiry warnings now appear 48 hours before renewal needed

entries.headline: Enhanced automation templates for better workflow creation
details: Updated skill templates for go-to-market planning and Salesforce workflows
Reorganized template library for easier discovery
Added missing template files for faster automation setup

entries.headline: Better Salesforce permissions management for workflow deployments
details: Enhanced validation ensures your Salesforce workflows have proper access rights
Automatic permission set assignment reduces manual setup steps
Improved field-level security handling for smoother data operations
